title: Battle Analysis Computer
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The '''Battle Analysis Computer''', or '''BAC''', was an onboard [[Starship/Legends|starship]] computer system designed by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] to provide starship [[Captain/Legends|captains]] and [[Fleet Commander/Legends|fleet commanders]] with tactical analyses, statistics and suggestions based on real-time feeds. If provided with accurate pre-battle inputs, such as estimated enemy strength and fleet logistics, the BAC could develop battle plans and predict outcomes. To enhance a commander's understanding of the multifaceted data output, the BAC could be connected to an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] for a real-time [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] projection of the battle.

==History==
The BAC prototype was originally supposed to be ready for the battle with the [[Death Star II/Legends|Second Death Star]], but software issues kept it inoperable. It was tested aboard ''[[Flurry]]'' by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] at the [[Invasion of Bakura]]. Though it proved quite useful to Skywalker, the testing also revealed several flaws, including a tendency to provide confusing or contradictory responses when confronted with rapidly changing battle conditions. Skywalker recommended adding a counterpart module to the BAC, possibly an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]], to assist in sifting through the data and to provide more useful recommendations with greater clarity. General Dodonna's staff, still intact despite the apparent death of the General years before, took Skywalker's field test information and began refining the software of the computer, with the expectation that the production version of the BAC would be installed on many of the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]' larger warships.

==Appearances==
*''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''
